title: `CLONE AS` fails when source and target tables are located in different databases
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
According to the documentation, `CREATE TABLE ... CLONE AS` supports different databases (https://clickhouse.com/docs/sql-reference/statements/create/table#with-a-schema-and-data-cloned-from-another-table):
```sql
CREATE TABLE [IF NOT EXISTS] [db.]table_name CLONE AS [db2.]name2 [ENGINE = engine]
```
However, query `CREATE TABLE db2.a CLONE AS db1.a` fails with error:
```
Received exception from server (version 25.11.1):
Code: 60.
DB::Exception: Received from localhost:9000.
DB::Exception: Table default.a does not exist. Maybe you meant db1.a?. (UNKNOWN_TABLE)
(query: CREATE TABLE db2.a CLONE AS db1.a;)
```
To be more precise, only the 2nd step (`ATTACH PARTITION`) fails - table `db2.a` is created successfully.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
Fiddle - https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/fcba0bf2-712c-48c6-829c-ba385ad3e8cf
```sql
CREATE DATABASE db1;
CREATE TABLE db1.a (id Int64) ENGINE=MergeTree ORDER BY tuple();
INSERT INTO db1.a VALUES (1), (2), (3);
SELECT count() FROM db1.a;
CREATE DATABASE db2;
CREATE TABLE db2.a CLONE AS db1.a;
-- Received exception from server (version 25.11.1):
-- Code: 60.
-- DB::Exception: Received from localhost:9000.
-- DB::Exception: Table default.a does not exist. Maybe you meant db1.a?. (UNKNOWN_TABLE)
-- (query: CREATE TABLE db2.a CLONE AS db1.a;)
```
### Expected behavior
_No response_
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE db1.a (id BIGINT, name VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE db2.a (id BIGINT, name VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO db2.a (id, name, created_at) SELECT id, name, created_at FROM db1.a;
key_bug_features: CLONE AS fails across different databases
